basics.name: Alexander Friedrichsen
basics.label: Data Scientist & Applied AI Engineer
basics.summary: Data scientist building production AI systems at the intersection of machine learning, automation, and software engineering. Drove AI adoption at a mutual insurance company — standing up production ML pipelines, agentic workflows, and a modern dev environment. Published ML researcher with experience shipping full-stack data products, ETL pipelines, and dashboards. Open to relocation (NYC, SF). Secret clearance.

work.name: Union Mutual
work.summary: Data scientist and AI lead at a mutual insurance company. Co-built production ML systems and drove organization-wide AI adoption.
highlights: Co-designed and deployed an AI-augmented inspection evaluation pipeline — computer vision model analyzes property and inspection images, rates risk, detects issues, and generates underwriter reports — reducing company losses by over $3M annually

Drove AI adoption company-wide as sole internal AI advocate: introduced version control (GitHub), established production vs. test environments, restructured codebases for agent integration, and delivered hands-on agentic coding workshops to technical and non-technical staff
Conduct exploratory modeling, feature engineering, and SQL querying across AWS (Redshift, S3) to support business decision-making and data accessibility
Automate dashboards and monthly reports in Power BI and Python, delivering actionable KPI insights to cross-functional stakeholders

work.name: BAE Systems
highlights: Built multi-source Power BI data models with DAX aggregations, delivering weekly dashboards that drove labor and product line decisions for senior stakeholders
Architected internal staffing solutions using SharePoint and MS Access, serving 16,000 employees
Spearheaded ETL pipeline integrating financial data, enabling transition from manual Excel analysis to predictive analytics

work.name: Social Ecological Gaming and Simulation Lab
highlights: Developed precision agriculture simulation software using Python, C#, and JavaScript in Unity Game Engine

work.name: Vermont Department of Health
highlights: Automated reporting workflows (SAS, R) and published data products for CDC reporting systems, saving 10+ hours per month

education.institution: University of Vermont, Complex Systems Center
University of Vermont, College of Engineering and Mathematics

publications.name: Evolutionary Machine Learning for Robust Facility Placements
publications.summary: Published research on evolutionary algorithms for optimization under uncertainty. Also presented at JuliaCon.

projects.name: Multi-Agent AI Orchestration System
projects.description: Built a production multi-agent system with 12 specialized AI agents coordinated through an Obsidian knowledge vault. Includes role-based task delegation, automated quality gates, persistent memory, MCP tool servers, and Discord-based always-on agent infrastructure.
highlights: 12 specialized agents with model routing (judgment/execution/support tiers)
Knowledge graph integration, automated vault search, session persistence
Custom hooks for quality enforcement, secret detection, and agent verification

projects.name: MTG Skill Analyzer
projects.description: Full-stack web application analyzing 20,000+ competitive Magic: The Gathering match outcomes. Deployed at honestafblog.com with Supabase backend.
highlights: ELO rating system, player performance modeling, data-driven team strategy
